command: dism /Mount-Wim /WimFile:C:\Images\install.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Install; dism /Image:C:\Mount\Install /Add-Package /PackagePath:C:\Payloads\BackdoorSvc.cab; dism /Unmount-Wim /MountDir:C:\Mount\Install /Commit; Get-FileHash C:\Images\install.wim -Algorithm SHA256; fc /b C:\Images\install_clean.wim C:\Images\install.wim
explanation: Adds a custom package to the offline Windows image to implant components, then validates the change via checksum and binary comparison against a baseline image.
rationale_steps: Mount the image to allow package injection.
Install a malicious CAB package into the system image.
Commit and unmount to finalize the system image patch.
Verify post-modification integrity with hashing and fc.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed DISM and PowerShell command chain with valid flags, paths, and separators; no quoting or parsing issues.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Appropriately mounts and modifies an offline Windows image consistent with T1601, but it requires admin privileges (not 'None') and the fc call in PowerShell resolves to Format-Custom rather than file compare, weakening the stated validation.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The commands are well-formed with valid DISM flags and PowerShell cmdlets, though mixing cmd-style (fc /b) and PowerShell commands in a single semicolon-chained sequence may cause issues since fc is a cmd built-in not natively available in PowerShell without cmd.exe invocation.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The sequence correctly demonstrates offline Windows image modification (mounting a WIM, injecting a backdoor package via CAB, committing changes) which aligns well with T1601 firmware/image tampering, and the checksum/binary comparison steps accurately reflect integrity validation against a baseline, though the 'Privilege: None' claim is incorrect as DISM mount and image modification operations require administrator privileges.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command string correctly uses semicolons to chain valid DISM, PowerShell, and cmd-builtin commands with appropriate flags and paths.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command sequence accurately performs an offline modification of a Windows image by mounting, injecting a package, and committing changes, which perfectly matches T1601.
